titleStmt.title: SII 27.360: original edition by M. D. Sampath, H. K. Narasimhamurthy, Madhav N. Katti and B. R. Gopal
No. 360. (A.R. No. 352 of 1930-31.) VIṬLA, PUTTUR TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.

summary.p: Copper sheet preserved in the palace.

The record states that Muṇḍi-seṭṭi-śēkhara had the golden pinnacle (kalaśa) set up on the temple of Pañchaliṅgadēva of Ishṭakapura, on behalf of Māṇimaida nephew (aḷiya) of Ḍoṁba-veggaḍe alias Kinyaṇṇakāva, who was cured of disease. The donor was himself the nephew of Evaṇa-svāmi son of Ḍoṁba-veggaḍe alias Kuṁñi-śēkhara.
It is dated Śaka 1358, Rākshasa, Mīna 17, Wednesday. The details are irregular. The tithi corresponds to 1436 A.D., March 12 when the weekday was Monday, the Śaka year being current.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
śrī sakavaruṣa 1358 ya rākṣasa saṁbatsarada mīnamāsa 17 bu Iṣṭakāpūrada śrī paṁcaliṁga dēvarige kuṁṅaśēkharā ḍoṁba veggaḍeyara maga vavaṇa svāmiya
ra Aḷiya muṁḍiseṭṭi śēkharu kinyaṁṇakāvarāda ḍoṁbuveggaḍeyara Aḷiya maṇimaidana vyādhiparihārārtthavāgi samarppisida suvarṇa kalaśake maṁgala mahāśrī
Digital edition of SII 27.360 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
